quiz.title: Unit 1 Quiz 9, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Advanced Lab Equipment Identification
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Laboratory Equipment Identification and Functions: Glassware, Heating Devices, Support Stands, and Separation Tools

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A beaker is a cylindrical container with a flat bottom and a pouring lip, used for holding, mixing, and heating liquids. It is less precise than graduated cylinders for volume measurement.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A standard funnel is used for transferring liquids or fine-grained materials into containers with small openings. It often holds filter paper for gravity filtration.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: Tongs are used to grasp hot containers such as beakers, crucibles, or test tubes. They provide a safe grip without direct hand contact with heated glassware.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A clamp attaches to a ring stand to securely hold glassware like burettes, condensers, or flasks in place during experiments.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A Bunsen burner produces a single open flame by mixing gas with air. It is used for heating, sterilization, and combustion reactions.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: An Erlenmeyer flask has a flat bottom, conical body, and narrow neck, making it ideal for swirling liquids without splashing. It is commonly used in titrations.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A crucible is a ceramic or metal container used for heating substances to very high temperatures, often with a lid. It is used in gravimetric analysis and ashing experiments.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A laboratory thermometer measures temperature changes during experiments. Unlike clinical thermometers, lab thermometers typically have a wider temperature range.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A hot plate provides controlled electrical heating without an open flame, making it safer for heating flammable liquids. Some models include magnetic stirring capabilities.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A retort stand (also called a ring stand) is a heavy metal base with a vertical rod used to support clamps and rings for holding laboratory glassware.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A tripod stand has three legs and is used to support beakers or flasks above a Bunsen burner. It is typically used with wire gauze to distribute heat evenly.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A spatula is used for transferring solid chemicals, scraping materials, or mixing. Spatulas come in various sizes and materials including stainless steel and porcelain.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A wash bottle is a squeeze bottle filled with distilled water or solvent used for rinsing glassware. It delivers a controlled stream of liquid when squeezed.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A Liebig condenser has a straight inner tube surrounded by an outer jacket for cooling water. It is used in distillation to cool and condense vapors back into liquid.

questions.question: The image below represents:
questions.explanation: A separatory funnel is used in liquid-liquid extraction to separate immiscible liquids into layers. It has a stopcock at the bottom for controlled draining of the denser layer.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You're a master of lab equipment identification! 🧪🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the differences between similar-looking glassware (flasks vs. beakers).
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on specialized equipment like condensers and separatory funnels.
scoreMessages.0: Please review all lab equipment images and their primary functions before proceeding.
